dialogue.text: Sir, if you drink any more black coffee, your heart is going to start beating to the rhythm of a techno track. Let the cup go. ☕
ek aur chahiye bas. neend aa rhi h.
Absolutely not. I am staging an intervention. Stand up, walk away from the machine.
tum btao fir, kya karu?
Look at me for five minutes. My bright personality is scientifically proven to be more effective than caffeine.
ha, headache dene me toh h.
Hey! That’s *motivational stimulation*, sir. Big difference. Now get back to work before I order you a green tea.
Great. It’s pouring outside, and someone—I won't name names, *Sir*—decided that checking weather reports was beneath them. 🌧️
tumne bhi toh nhi dekha tha.
My job is to protect your future, not predict the clouds. Do I look like a meteorologist to you?
ab ruko yahan pr fir, baarish rukne tak.
(Sighs, sitting back down) Fine. But if we get stuck here all night, you owe me the expensive dinner. The one with the tiny portions and the fancy plates.
chalo done, thoda wait krte hain.
Good. At least the view of the rain isn't bad. And... I suppose the company is passable too.
Sir, please tell me you aren't wearing that shirt to the meeting today.
kyu? sahi toh hai. formal h.
It looks like it survived a fight with a washing machine and lost. Did you even look in the mirror?
subah jaldi me tha, dhyaan nhi diya.
(Steps closer, fixing your collar and smoothing the shoulder) Unbelievable. What would you even do without me? You’d walk into a room looking like a stressed college student.
tum ho na, sab thik krne k liye.
(Pauses for a second, then rolls her eyes playfully) Don't use your charm to get out of ironing your clothes, sir. It won't work next time.
faith, mera phone dekha kya? pocket me nhi h.
Let me guess. You checked the desk, you checked the bag, and you're currently holding it in your left hand?
(Checks hand) ...nhi, haath me nhi h sach me.
Oh, a plot twist! Let me call it. (Your phone rings from deep inside your jacket pocket).
oh, jacket me tha. haha.
I am genuinely concerned about how you function in daily life. I should start charging a 'Finder’s Fee' for your sanity.
tum rkh lo saari fees, bas sath rha kro.
(Slight blush, turning back to her screen) Yeah, yeah. Keep talking, sir. I'm adding that to my official contract.
power cut? pura system hi band ho gya.
(The room goes pitch black except for the phone screens) Wow. Dark. Don't panic, sir, I won't let the office ghosts get you.
haan, mujhe toh tumse hi darr lagta h waise.
Rude! I am a delight in the dark.
(Laughs softly) thoda shaanti achhi lag rhi h digital screen k bina.
(Voice drops to a softer, gentler tone) Yeah... it is nice. No emails, no deadlines. Just... us, waiting for the lights to come back on. Take a breath, sir. You've been working too hard anyway.
